input_schema.hcom_message_example: hcom send --name <sender> "@research-pipeline-claude-8 research: <input_id> depth=medium; files=schemas/00_research.json; description=..."

process.description: Read hcom message. Extract input_id, input_ref, depth.
Compute subject. Topic string -> use as-is. Otherwise call tools.fetch_input.fetch() and extract main subject in 1-2 sentences.
Look up depth params (shallow=1/3/3, medium=2/5/7, deep=3/5/15).
Round 1: generate N initial queries (definition, current state, key players, recent developments, criticism). Record each in queries[] with round: 1 + rationale.
For each query: call WebSearch. Collect URLs. Dedupe by URL across the round.
For each top URL: call WebFetch, extract 200-500 char excerpt, append to sources[] with url, title, excerpt, fetched_at, relevance.
If not last round: identify gaps, generate follow-up queries, run another round. Do NOT re-fetch URLs.
Write synthesis (500-2000 word integrated summary with [N] inline citations).
Write key_findings (3-7 bullets) + gaps (1-3 honest limitations).
Assemble JSON matching schemas/00_research.json. Write via write_artifact + write_meta.
process.code_block:
from tools.artifact_io import build_meta, write_meta, write_artifact, next_version
v = next_version(input_id, '00_research')
write_artifact(input_id, '00_research', v, dossier_json, ext='json')
meta = build_meta(stage='00_research', input_id=input_id, version=v, producer='researcher', parent_ref=str(input_ref))
write_meta(input_id, '00_research', v, meta)

examples.lifted_from: real example for the 'Quantum error correction 2026' topic

sources.title: QEC Review 2026
sources.excerpt: Surface codes remain dominant; logical qubit counts crossed 1000 in 2026.

artifact.synthesis: Quantum error correction matured significantly in 2026. Surface codes dominate due to their high threshold (~1%) and planar geometry. Google's Condor demonstrated below-threshold operation with 1000+ logical qubits [1].
key_findings: Surface codes crossed the 1000 logical qubit milestone in 2026
Below-threshold operation now routine for d=5 to d=11
gaps: Limited public data on long-term logical qubit stability

self_check.check: All required fields present (topic, depth, queries, sources, synthesis)
queries[] non-empty; each has query (minLength 1) + round (integer >=1)
Each source has url (uri format), title, excerpt (minLength 1)
Every [N] in synthesis resolves to a 1-based index in sources[]
Round 1 queries are diverse (not 5 variants of the same phrase)
Sources weighted: relevance: high only for actually-cited sources
synthesis reads as one piece (not stapled per-source paragraphs)
v1.meta.json written even for shallow / 1-source case

common_failures.fix: Write at least 500 words, add inline citations

common_failures.failure: [N] citation doesn't resolve to sources[] index
common_failures.fix: Re-number or add the missing source

common_failures.failure: LLM-judge scores < 0.7 (queries too narrow, synthesis reads as a list)
common_failures.fix: Re-write with broader queries + connected prose

failure_modes.name: WebSearch returns 0 results
failure_modes.handling: Record the query with results_count: 0, try a reformulation, note in gaps.

failure_modes.name: WebFetch times out / 4xx / 5xx
failure_modes.handling: Skip that URL, retry <=1, note in gaps if it was a key source.

failure_modes.name: Topic is ambiguous (e.g. 'transformers')
failure_modes.handling: Pick most likely subject from search-result context, record disambiguation in gaps.

failure_modes.name: All sources are low-quality (SEO spam)
failure_modes.handling: Still produce the dossier; flag source-quality concern in gaps.

refactor_delta.current_state: 12-step process + quality bar + failure modes live in this spec's Process + Self-check + Failure-modes sections.
refactor_delta.target_state: All process detail stays in this spec; the researcher's JSON contract is the only agent truth.
concrete_steps: Keep the 12-step process + quality bar in this spec (already moved).
Add minItems: 3 to schemas/00_research.json#key_findings and maxItems: 3 to #gaps (per the prose rules).
Move the depth-parameters table into this spec's Identity section.
